_meta.keyKnowledge: We are learning to evaluate and use data and information effectively to address economic, business, work, or financial issues.

_source.w: We are learning to evaluate and use data and information effectively to address economic, business, work, or financial issues.
y: Evaluation ensures we use only the most reliable and relevant information in our analysis.
The ability to use data effectively — not just collect it — is what produces actionable insights.
These skills prepare us for evidence-based work in business, policy, academia, and everyday decision-making.
eg: Evaluating the reliability and currency of economic data before using it in our analysis.
Using statistical data to build a case for or against a particular economic policy.
Applying data to address a real-world financial issue — like evaluating investment options or budget trade-offs.
Discussing the limitations of the data available and how this affects our conclusions.
Using multiple data sources to triangulate our findings and strengthen our analysis.
Presenting data-driven recommendations for an economic or business issue.
